message:
feat(learn): replace markdown with custom React lesson components

Complete overhaul of the Learning Hub lesson system:
- Remove all ReactMarkdown/rehype rendering
- Create 9 custom lesson components with stunning UI
- Add glassmorphism, animations, and premium visual polish
- Build reusable lesson-components.tsx primitives
- Update routing to use custom components instead of markdown files

This replaces plaintext markdown rendering with world-class UI:
- GoalBanner, Section, CodeBlock, TipBox, FeatureCard, etc.
- Framer Motion animations throughout
- Interactive keyboard shortcut grids
- Visual diagrams and connection flows
- Gradient effects and hover states

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

@@ -0,0 +1,392 @@ +"use client"; + +import { motion } from "@/components/motion"; +import { + Palette, + Sparkles, + Code2, + FileText, + Bug, + TestTube, + Layers, + Send, + FolderOpen, + Lightbulb, + Play, +} from "lucide-react"; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + InlineCode, + BulletList, +} from "./lesson-components"; + +export function NtmPaletteLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Discover the pre-built prompts that supercharge your agents. + + + {/* What Is The Command Palette */} +
} + delay={0.1} + > + + NTM ships with a command palette - a collection + of battle-tested prompts for common development tasks. + + + These aren't just prompts. They're carefully crafted + instructions that get the best results from coding agents. + + +
+ +
+ + This opens an interactive browser of all available prompts. + +
+ + + + {/* Palette Categories */} +
} + delay={0.15} + > + The prompts are organized into categories: + +
+ } + title="Architecture & Design" + items={[ + "System design analysis", + "Architecture review", + "API design patterns", + ]} + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + delay={0.1} + /> + } + title="Code Quality" + items={[ + "Code review prompts", + "Refactoring suggestions", + "Bug hunting strategies", + ]} + gradient="from-sky-500/20 to-blue-500/20" + delay={0.2} + /> + } + title="Testing" + items={[ + "Test generation", + "Coverage analysis", + "Edge case discovery", + ]} + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + delay={0.3} + /> + } + title="Documentation" + items={[ + "README generation", + "API documentation", + "Inline comment review", + ]} + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + delay={0.4} + /> + } + title="Debugging" + items={[ + "Error analysis", + "Performance profiling", + "Memory leak detection", + ]} + gradient="from-red-500/20 to-rose-500/20" + delay={0.5} + /> +
+
+ + + + {/* Using Palette Prompts */} +
} + delay={0.2} + > +
+ Open the palette: ntm palette, + "Select a prompt", + "Copy it", + <>Use ntm send or paste directly, + ]} + /> + + +
+ +
+

+ This lets you select a prompt and immediately send it to all + agents! +

+ +

@@ -0,0 +1,423 @@ +"use client"; + +import { motion } from "@/components/motion"; +import { + Key, + Lock, + Wifi, + Shield, + RefreshCw, + Terminal, + CheckCircle2, + HelpCircle, + Laptop, + Server, + ArrowRight, +} from "lucide-react"; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + CommandList, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + InlineCode, + FeatureGrid, + FeatureCard, +} from "./lesson-components"; + +export function SSHBasicsLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Understand how to stay connected to your VPS. + + + {/* What Is SSH */} +
} + delay={0.1} + > + + SSH (Secure Shell) is how you're connected to + this VPS right now. + + + It's an encrypted tunnel between your laptop and this server. + + + {/* Visual Connection Diagram */} +
+ +
+
+ + + + {/* How You Got Here */} +
} + delay={0.15} + > + + Your VPS connection happened in two stages: + + + {/* Stage Cards */} +
+ + +
+ + {/* Command Breakdown */} +
+

+ Breaking down the command: +

+
+ + + + +
+
+
+ + + + {/* If Connection Drops */} +
} + delay={0.2} + > + + No worries! SSH connections drop sometimes. Just reconnect—your work + is safe in tmux (next lesson). + +
+ + + + {/* SSH Keys vs Passwords */} +
} + delay={0.25} + > + + You're now using key-based authentication: + + +
+ + } + title="Private Key" + description="Stays on your laptop at ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Public Key" + description="Lives on the VPS at ~/.ssh/authorized_keys" + gradient="from-sky-500/20 to-blue-500/20" + /> + +
+ +
+ + This is more secure than passwords and lets you connect without + typing anything. + +
+
+ + + + {/* Keeping Connections Alive */} +
} + delay={0.3} + > + + Add this to your laptop's ~/.ssh/config: + + +
+ +
+ + This sends keepalive packets every 60 seconds. +
+ + + + {/* Quick Connect Alias */} +
} + delay={0.35} + > + + On your laptop, add to ~/.zshrc or{" "} + ~/.bashrc: + + +
+ +
+ + + Then just type vps to connect! + +
+ + + + {/* Verify Section */} +
} + delay={0.4} + > + +
+ + + + {/* Practice Commands */} +
} + delay={0.45} + > + + Try these commands to confirm your SSH setup is working: + + +
+ +
+ +
+ + When you see your public key (starts with{" "} + ssh-ed25519), you know the setup worked! + +
+
+
+ ); +} + +// ============================================================================= +// CONNECTION DIAGRAM - Visual SSH connection +// ============================================================================= +function ConnectionDiagram() { + return ( +

+ Never lose work when SSH drops. + + {/* What Is tmux */} +

} + delay={0.25} + > + , + }, + { + keys: ["Ctrl+a", "-"], + action: "Split horizontally", + icon: , + }, + { + keys: ["Ctrl+a", "h/j/k/l"], + action: "Move between panes", + icon: , + }, + { + keys: ["Ctrl+a", "x"], + action: "Close current pane", + icon: , + }, + ]} + /> +

+ + + + {/* Copy Mode */} +
} + delay={0.35} + > + , + }, + { + keys: ["j/k", "or arrows"], + action: "Scroll", + icon: , + }, + { keys: ["q"], action: "Exit copy mode", icon: }, + { keys: ["v"], action: "Start selection", icon: }, + { keys: ["y"], action: "Copy selection", icon: }, + ]} + /> +

+

{title}
